mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases in Kubernetes environments. It automates the operational lifecycle of PostgreSQL, making it easier to deploy, manage, and maintain databases. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native features for high availability.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

```

> This repository contains the CloudNativePG Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the CloudNativePG operator to manage PostgreSQL clusters effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.29.0/deploy/crds/cloudnative-pg.io_clusters.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator using Helm, which will manage PostgreSQL clusters.

```bash

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0 --namespace cloudnative-pg --create-namespace

```

> This command installs the operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> This command creates a 3-instance PostgreSQL cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> You should see your cluster listed with a status of 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all PostgreSQL clusters managed by the operator, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> This command uninstalls the operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0 --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backup existing cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the operator.
> Verify that the clusters are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD is not installed correctly or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CRD is installed and check your resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if one or more instances of the cluster are failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the PostgreSQL pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cannot connect to PostgreSQL'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate network issues or that the PostgreSQL service is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n cloudnative-pg' and check pod logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct role bindings.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME              READY   STATUS   AGE' with your PostgreSQL cluster listed and a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
